song.title: It Passeth Knowledge
song.lyrics:
[V1] 1) It passeth knowledge, That dear love of Thine, My Savior, Jesus; Yet this soul of mine Would of Thy love in all Its breadth and length, Its height and depth, Its everlasting strength, Know more and more.
[V2] 2) It passeth telling, That dear love of Thine, My Savior, Jesus; Yet these lips of mine Would fain proclaim to sinners, Far and near, A love which can remove all guilty fear, And love beget.
[V3] 3) It passeth praises, That dear love of Thine, My Savior, Jesus; Yet this heart of mine Would sing that love, So full, so rich, so free, Which brings a rebel sinner, such as me, Nigh unto God.
[V4] 4) But though I cannot sing, Or tell, or know The fulness of Thy love, While here below, My empty vessel I may freely bring; O Thou, who art of love the living spring, My vessel fill.
[V5] 5) I am an empty vessel—not one thought Or look of love to Thee I’ve ever brought; Yet I may come, And come again to Thee, With this the empty sinner’s only plea, Thou lovest me.
[V6] 6) Oh, fill me, Jesus, Savior, with Thy love! Lead, lead me to the living fount above; Thither may I, in simple faith draw nigh, And never to another fountain fly, But unto Thee.
[V7] 7) Lord Jesus, when Thee Face to face I see, When on Thy lofty throne I sit with Thee, Then of Thy love, In all its breadth and length, Its height and depth, Its everlasting strength, My soul shall sing.
song.author: Mary Shekleton, Ira David Sankey
